system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

vertices

    (0    0.02 0)        // 3

    (0.02 0    0.001)    // 5
    (0.02 0.02 0.001)    // 6
    (0    0.02 0.001)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

    solver          ode;

}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/2/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/2/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 47.

    From function const Foam::dictionary& Foam::dictionary::subDict(const Foam::word&) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 1008.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same `species`-related error that has persisted across 7 attempts. Despite numerous structural variations to `physicalProperties` (flat species, `specie` sub-dictionary, nested `species` inside `specie`, removing `chemistryReader` from `thermo.compressibleGas`), the error remains. 

Looking at the current state carefully, I notice that in **Attempt 7**, the `physicalProperties` file has `inertSpecie N2;` followed by a `species` list and a `specie` sub-dictionary. Yet the error still says `CH4 is undefined`. This strongly suggests that in OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture` with `hePsiThermo`, the solver is looking for **individual species entries** (like `CH4`, `O2`, etc.) as **direct top-level keywords** in `physicalProperties`, not inside any sub-dictionary.

In other words, the solver is doing something equivalent to `lookUp("CH4")` on the `physicalProperties` dictionary directly, and it expects to find a `CH4` entry at the same level as `inertSpecie`, `species`, and `specie`.

## Root Cause

After reviewing the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case structure and considering the persistent failure, I conclude that OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture` with `hePsiThermo` expects each species to have its **own top-level entry** in `physicalProperties` with its molecular weight value. The `species` list serves as the lookup key set, and the solver tries to find each species name as a direct dictionary entry.

The correct format for OpenFOAM 10 is therefore:

```
inertSpecie N2;

CH4       12.011;
O2        31.998;
N2        28.014;
H2O       18.015;
CO2       44.01;
```

That is, **remove the `species` list entirely** and **remove the `specie` sub-dictionary**. Simply list each species with its molecular weight as a flat top-level entry in `physicalProperties`.

### 1. Rewrite `constant/physicalProperties` — Flat species entries

Remove the `species` list and the `specie { ... }` block. Replace them with individual species entries at the top level:

```
inertSpecie N2;

CH4       12.011;
O2        31.998;
N2        28.014;
H2O       18.015;
CO2       44.01;
```

Keep everything else (`thermoType`, `energy`, `equationOfState`, etc.) unchanged.

### 2. Clean `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Remove any remaining deprecated entries (`chemistryReader`, `foamChemistryFile`, `foamChemistryThermoFile`). Keep it minimal with just the `thermoType` block.

### 3. Leave other files unchanged

The `reactions`, `chemistryProperties`, and all boundary condition files appear correct. No changes needed there.

### Summary

The solution is to abandon the `species` list and `specie` sub-dictionary approach entirely. In OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture` with `hePsiThermo`, the solver looks up each species name (e.g., `CH4`) as a **direct top-level entry** in `physicalProperties` and expects to find its molecular weight there. Previous attempts tried various sub-dictionary structures, but the actual requirement is simpler: flat species entries at the top level.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.2 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/physicalProperties', changes='Remove the species list entirely; Remove the specie sub-dictionary block; Add flat top-level entries for each species with their molecular weights: CH4 12.011, O2 31.998, N2 28.014, H2O 18.015, CO2 44.01; Keep inertSpecie N2; Keep all other entries unchanged')]
